reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into Spanish, preserving frontmatter keys, only translating reader-facing values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, cover_credit (none present). Keep other metadata unchanged. Change language field to "Spanish". Also need to prefix relative URLs with ../ but there are none? The frontmatter includes social_image etc. Those are filenames; they are not URLs. No relative URLs in body. There's a

tag; keep as is. We must preserve code blocks; there are none. Keep markdown headings: there is none besides frontmatter. The body is plain paragraphs. Need to translate preserving paragraph breaks. Also note the asterisk footnote: "*Assuming we can improve the environmental impact, and avoid a dystopian nightmare. But that's a topic for another day." It is preceded by \* _Assuming..._ So we need to translate that text, preserving the markdown formatting: \* _..._ Keep the backslash? In original it's "\* _Assuming we can improve the environmental impact, and avoid a dystopian nightmare. But that's a topic for another day._". The backslash before asterisk is to escape? We'll keep same pattern: \* _..._ with translated Spanish inside. Also need to keep the emphasis markup: **text** etc. Keep them. Let's translate: Frontmatter: language: English -> Spanish: "Spanish" title: "The Last to Think" -> translate: "Los últimos en pensar" maybe. Keep quotes. Should be Spanish translation: "Los últimos en pensar". Keep same quotes. subTitle: Human or Machine? -> "¿Humano o máquina?" Keep question mark. Other fields unchanged. Now body: "Every generation discovers its technological bogeyman. Some new device emerges, and suddenly civilization teeters on the brink of idiocy. You could set your Apple Watch by these panics—assuming it has a charge. Lest we resort to analog timekeeping." Translate: "Cada generación descubre su monstruo tecnológico. Aparece un nuevo dispositivo y, de repente, la civilización se tambalea al borde de la idiotez. Podrías sincronizar tu Apple Watch con estos temores—suponiendo que tenga batería. Antes de que nos veamos obligados a recurrir a la medición de tiempo analógica." Make sure to keep dash em dash. "Today's panic centers on AI, but before we hyperventilate about 12-year-olds who couldn't write an analog 'thank you' note yet can "write" a deep philosophical paper on the themes of justice and redemption in *The Brothers Karamazov*, let's check our historical receipts." Translate: "La preocupación actual gira en torno a la IA, pero antes de hiperventilar por niños de 12 años que no podían escribir una nota de agradecimiento analógica y ya pueden “escribir” un profundo ensayo filosófico sobre los temas de justicia y redención en *Los hermanos Karamazov*, revisemos nuestros antecedentes históricos." Note: preserve italics for *The Brothers Karamazov*; original uses *The Brothers Karamazov* (italic). Keep same. "From CliffsNotes to calculators, each new technology has sparked fears of intellectual decay. In the 1940s, educators warned that CliffsNotes would make students lazy readers. In the 1950s, comic books were blamed for juvenile delinquency. The 1980s saw calculators branded as "crutches that will hobble a generation." The internet was once feared as the end of attention spans, yet it democratized knowledge and connected rural students to world-class resources. Each time, society adapted rather than collapsed." Translate: "Desde los CliffsNotes hasta las calculadoras, cada nueva tecnología ha despertado temores de decadencia intelectual. En la década de 1940, los educadores advertían que los CliffsNotes convertirían a los estudiantes en lectores perezosos. En los años 50, los cómics fueron culpados de la delincuencia juvenil. En los 80, las calculadoras fueron catalogadas como “apoyos que coartan a una generación”. En su momento, se temía que Internet fuera el fin de la capacidad de atención, pero democratizó el conocimiento y conectó a estudiantes rurales con recursos de clase mundial. Cada vez, la sociedad se adaptó en lugar de colapsar." Note: preserve quotes for "crutches that will hobble a generation." Keep same double quotes. "Consider GPS—the doomsayers were technically right. Most people under 40 can't read paper maps. But instead of becoming wayward travellers, we gained the ability to navigate more often, in any city worldwide, all while avoiding traffic. Who is hand-wringing about the loss of map-reading skills now? No one. We adapted." Translate: "Consideremos el GPS—los profetas del fin tenían razón en lo técnico. La mayoría de las personas menores de 40 años no saben leer mapas de papel. Pero en lugar de convertirnos en viajeros perdidos, ganamos la capacidad de orientarnos con mayor frecuencia, en cualquier ciudad del mundo, evitando el tráfico. ¿Quién se lamenta ahora por la pérdida de la habilidad de leer mapas? Nadie. Nos adaptamos." "AI will surely follow this pattern.* Yes, it can process vast information and generate human-like text at inhuman speeds. No, it can't replicate human emotional intelligence or creativity. The challenge isn't preventing its use but **teaching its effective application**, building literacy around it. **Students need to understand** when AI amplifies learning versus short-circuits development. **Educators must redesign assessments** for an AI-augmented world." Translate: "La IA seguramente seguirá este patrón.* Sí, puede procesar información masiva y generar texto similar al humano a velocidades sobrehumanas. No, no puede replicar la inteligencia emocional ni la creatividad humana. El desafío no es impedir su uso sino **enseñar su aplicación eficaz**, construyendo alfabetización en torno a ella. **Los estudiantes deben comprender** cuándo la IA potencia el aprendizaje versus cuando corta el desarrollo. **Los educadores deben rediseñar las evaluaciones** para un mundo potenciado por IA." "We simply can't expect the old methods to work in this new landscape." Translate: "Simplemente no podemos esperar que los métodos antiguos funcionen en este nuevo panorama." "We survived calculators, CliffsNotes, and (so far) TikTok. We'll survive ChatGPT. The kids will adapt. I hope the adults don't take too long to catch up." Translate: "Sobrevivimos a las calculadoras, a los CliffsNotes y (hasta ahora) a TikTok. Sobreviviremos a ChatGPT. Los niños se adaptarán. Espero que los adultos no tarden demasiado en ponerse al día."

keep. Footnote: "\* _Assuming we can improve the environmental impact, and avoid a dystopian nightmare. But that's a topic for another day._" Translate: "\* _Suponiendo que podamos mejorar el impacto ambiental y evitar una pesadilla distópica. Pero ese es un tema para otro día._" Make sure to keep backslash before asterisk. Now produce final output with frontmatter and body. Check frontmatter: need to keep same order. language: Spanish. title: "Los últimos en pensar". subTitle: "¿Humano o máquina?" Keep quotes. Everything else unchanged. Now produce final answer.
